response:
```python
# test_email_validator.py
import pytest

from your_module import is_valid_email  # replace with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        "user@example.com",
        "USER123@example.COM",
        "user.name+tag@example.co.uk",
        "user_name%tag-1@example-domain.org",
        "a@b.co",  # minimal valid shape with 2-char TLD
        "first.last@sub.domain.museum",  # long TLD and subdomains
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_returns_true_for_valid_addresses(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        "",
        " ",
        "plainaddress",
        "missing-at.example.com",
        "@example.com",
        "user@",
        "user@example",
        "user@example.",
        "user@example.c",  # TLD too short
        "user@example.123",  # numeric TLD not allowed by regex
        "user@@example.com",
        "user name@example.com",
        "user@example_com.com",  # underscore in domain
        " user@example.com",
        "user@example.com ",
        "üser@example.com",  # non-ASCII local part
        "user@exämple.com",  # non-ASCII domain
        "user@[192.168.1.1]",  # not supported by this regex
        '"user"@example.com',  # quoted local part not supported
        "user@example.com\nextra",
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_returns_false_for_invalid_addresses(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "bad_input",
    [
        None,
        123,
        12.5,
        [],
        {},
        b"user@example.com",
        object(),
    ],
)
def test_is_valid_email_raises_type_error_for_non_string_inputs(bad_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        is_valid_email(bad_input)  # type: ignore[arg-type]


# Optional: these document stricter email-validity edge cases that the current
# regex accepts. Keep as xfail until the implementation is hardened.
@pytest.mark.xfail(
    strict=True,
    reason="Current regex accepts these common invalid email forms.",
)
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "email",
    [
        ".user@example.com",
        "user.@example.com",
        "user..name@example.com",
        "user@example..com",
        "user@.example.com",
        "user@-example.com",
        "user@example-.com",
        "user@example.com\n",  # accepted because `$` can match before final newline
    ],
)
def test_stricter_invalid_addresses_should_be_rejected(email):
    assert is_valid_email(email) is False
```
